title: World
description: A curated Polis world (the substrate's outermost container). Worlds are NOT user-creatable; the staq curates them. Each world defines the geographic basis, calendar / time mapping, physics, default economic posture, moderation floor, asset pack, and the cognition corpora that ground AI characters in this world's contemporary context. v0.1.2 amends calendar per DECISION-TIME-MODEL.md: in_world_day_per_real_day default bumped 1.0 → 12.0 (one canonical clock at 12× wall-clock for Modern Earth 2026); adds heartbeat_world_seconds (default 3600) for WorldWorkflow emergent-state checks between scheduled events. **v0.1.3 (2026-05-23, P2 Phase A.5) ADDITIVE per P1 D3+D4 substrate-vs-content boundary:** adds calendar.needs_axes — the per-world list of essentials axes this world declares. SHAPE is substrate (every world has a needs dict on character.state.needs); AXES are world-content (which axes apply to chars in THIS world). Modern Earth 2026 declares [hunger, fatigue, shelter, social]; future worlds (Mars-colony V2+) declare different axes ([atmospheric_pressure, radiation_dose, oxygen, social]) without schema migration. Empty array = world doesn't run needs primitive (e.g., Devnet test worlds).

id.description: Slug identifier; unique across all Polis worlds.

name.description: Human-readable world name (e.g. 'Modern Earth 2026', 'Cyberpunk 2099'). MUST NOT be a real-world place verbatim.

description.description: Editorial framing — what this world is, what makes it distinct, what kind of stories belong here.

era.description: Era classification. Affects which industries / professions / institutions can spawn.

tech_level.description: Free-form era descriptor (e.g. '2026', '2099', '1200', '2150'). Used by AI characters as world-state context.

source.description: Geographic basis. real-earth uses OSM verbatim; earth-derived uses OSM with transformations (sea-level rise, megacity overlays); fictional uses authored GeoJSON; abstract has no map.

available_regions.description: 'all' (full world available) or a list of region IDs that countries may spawn within.

tile_style_uri.description: Optional custom MapLibre style URI. Substrate-fictional aesthetic.

calendar.description: World time configuration. Per DECISION-TIME-MODEL.md: Polis runs on a single canonical world clock per world that advances at a uniform ratio vs wall-clock. No per-viewer scaling, no fast-forward. The kernel emits events at world-time pace.

in_world_day_per_real_day.description: BOUND: time mapping. 1.0 means in-world day = 1 real day (EVE-style 1:1); 12.0 = 1 real-hour produces 12 world-hours = half a world-day (Polis V0 default per DECISION-TIME-MODEL.md, Modern Earth 2026); 24.0 = 1 world-day per real-hour (fast-cycle); 48.0 = PRD §22 directional example (rejected as too aggressive for character life-arc texture). Recommended range per V0 decision: [8.0, 24.0]. Capped to prevent meta-cycle attacks.

heartbeat_world_seconds.description: Per DECISION-TIME-MODEL.md §3.3: how often WorldWorkflow's heartbeat fires (in world-seconds) to check for emergent state between scheduled events. Default 3600 (1 world-hour ≈ 5 real-minutes at 12× rate). Lower values increase CPU + Temporal-timer cost; higher values mean emergent state lags.

needs_axes.description: Per P1 substrate-design-locked.md D3+D4 (v0.1.3 add): list of essentials-axis names this world declares. AXES are world-content per D3 substrate-vs-content boundary. character.state.needs is a substrate primitive (every world has it); AXES per this list. Modern Earth 2026 declares [hunger, fatigue, shelter, social]; future worlds (Mars-colony V2+) declare domain-appropriate axes. EMPTY = world doesn't run needs primitive (test/devnet worlds). Per P1 lint rule #2: substrate code MAY NOT hardcode axis names — always read from world.config.yaml.needs_axes.

current_in_world_date.description: Current in-world date. Advances per the mapping above.

physics.description: World-level physics constraints. Affects transport, contract delivery, character action latency.

travel_speed.description: Maximum substrate-realistic transport speed. Constrains contract delivery + character relocation.

communication.description: How fast information travels in this world. Affects press recap latency, character coordination, market updates.

custom_constants.description: World-specific physics constants (e.g. mana availability for high-fantasy, bandwidth tiers for cyberpunk).

primary_currency.description: World-level default. Countries within can issue their own currency on top (per country.schema.json).

default_inflation.description: BOUND: world-baseline inflation. Countries can override within their own bounds.

default_taxation_posture.description: Whether taxation is per-country (default) or world-imposes a floor / forbids taxation.

asset_pack.description: Visual / audio assets that ship with this world.

base_3d_uri.description: IPFS URI to base 3D models (buildings, vehicles, generic props).

fonts.description: Web fonts available in this world's UI. Substrate-fictional but world-thematic.

tile_aesthetic.description: Map tile style override (e.g. cyberpunk-neon, woodcut-editorial, satellite-photographic).

floor.description: Moderation floor. NEVER overrides engine Tier 1 hard floors (CSAM, real threats, sanctions); only configures Tier 2 latitude (violence, adult content, etc.).

violence_allowed.description: Sport violence, war contexts. CSAM and real-person threats are always banned regardless.

real_world_political_references.description: Per GUARDRAILS D3: how strict is the LLM-judge on real-world political/national references.

age_gate_world_min.description: BOUND: minimum age for any user entering this world. 18 default.

age_gate.description: Convenience field — mirrors moderation.age_gate_world_min for backward compatibility.

available_languages.description: BCP-47 language codes available for in-world content. V0 default ['en']; V1+ expands.

visibility.description: public — anyone can join (subject to KYC tier if real-money). invite_only — closed alpha shape. private — staff-only. demo — read-only display world.

launch_date.description: Real-world date this world went live (or planned).

countries_allowed.description: Governance over what countries can spawn in this world.

user_creatable.description: V0: false (only the-staq curates countries). V1+: true once GUARDRAILS gates are battle-tested.

max_countries.description: Soft cap on how many countries can exist in this world.

real_earth_country_naming.description: Modern Earth allows 'England-on-Polis' (transparent reference, substrate-fictional). 'Cyberpunk 2099' might allow more invention. 'Tolkien-style high fantasy' refuses real-Earth references entirely.

industries_allowed.description: Governance over what industries can spawn in this world.

user_creatable.description: V0: false. V1+: true once industry creation gates are defined.

era_constraint.description: If true, industries must match the world's era (no semiconductors in medieval; no jousting in cyberpunk). LLM-judge enforces at industry-creation time.

mortality.description: World-level mortality model controls. Composes with country.actuarial_life_expectancy and the canonical mortality.schema.json catalogue. Per polis/MORTALITY-DESIGN.md.

v0_severity.description: Same enum as mortality.schema.json. Permissive at V0 — deaths rare; mostly acute or long-arc chronic.

suicide_safeguarding_policy.description: engine_gated_v0 = engine refuses death_event.cause: suicide without explicit safeguarding_signoff_id.

needs_calibration.description: Per-need decay / threshold / floor parameters. Applied uniformly to all characters in this world; per-character offsets via lifestyle / wealth / institution-role.

cognition.description: Per-world retrieval index (per V0-PLAN §3.10 5-layer composition). Light corpus — contemporary-context priors. Per-character / per-institution / per-industry / per-country corpora layered on top at retrieval time.

context.description: World-context priors (e.g. 'Modern Earth 2026 has mobile internet, current real-world geopolitical realities, AI-assisted everything'; 'Cyberpunk 2099 has megacorps, body augmentation, etc.')

founder_curated_by.description: Identifier of the curator (V0: 'the-staq'). Worlds are NEVER user-created at V0; this field acknowledges curatorial provenance.
